definition: Application Programming Interface — a set of protocols for building and integrating software
The time delay between a request and the start of a response
A temporary storage layer that speeds up repeated data access
An intermediary server that forwards requests between clients and servers
Server-Sent Events — a protocol for pushing real-time updates from server to browser
Internet Content Adaptation Protocol — used by proxies to transform HTTP content
A numerical vector representation of text that captures semantic meaning
Retrieval-Augmented Generation — enriching LLM prompts with retrieved context
A cache that matches queries by meaning similarity rather than exact match
A proxy technique for decrypting and inspecting HTTPS traffic using a trusted CA certificate
